reviewer: reviewer (sonnet) — independent of fixer ab5d14f and its plan-reviewer

commits_reviewed.diff_range: git diff 3160716 323464d (cumulative), git diff b1d7a7f 323464d (finalize-only, verified to be a pure 0024->0025 file rename + one comment-line edit, no other changes)

offers_persist_user_scoped: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE: apps/api/app/services/offers.py create_offer()/delete_offer()/_manual_offers() all scope by "userId" = %s (lines 156-159, 253-258, 273-276). Router POST /workspaces/offers and DELETE /workspaces/offers/{id} (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py) both require CurrentUser (401 without auth, asserted by test_post_offer_requires_auth / test_delete_offer_requires_auth). Ran apps/api/tests/test_offers_persist.py + test_workspaces.py fresh against DATABASE_URL_TEST this run: 19 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. test_post_persists_and_survives_get proves a POSTed offer is returned by a subsequent independent GET on both /workspaces/offers and /offers (real DB round-trip, not client state). test_offers_are_user_scoped registers a second real user and asserts the first user's offer id is absent from the second user's GET. test_delete_other_users_offer_is_404 proves cross-tenant delete returns 404 (not 403, not 200) — anti-enumeration correct — and the owner's offer is confirmed still present afterward.
migration_additive_0025_no_dup: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE: apps/api/migrations/0025_offers.sql contains only CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "Offer" (...) and CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS — no DROP, no ALTER TYPE, no rename. Confirmed via `ls apps/api/migrations/` on current main that 0024_email_thread_ai_score.sql is the sole 0024 file and there is no 0025 file yet on main, so no collision when this branch merges. `git log --all --diff-filter=A --name-only` shows exactly one file ever added as 0024_offers.sql (in b1d7a7f, on this branch, later renamed to 0025 in 323464d) and one file added as 0024_email_thread_ai_score.sql on a separate line of history — confirmed these are different commits/lineages, not a collision. Advisory lock: services/offers.py uses 7420240724 (_OFFERS_LOCK). Cross-checked against apps/api/app/repositories/background_jobs.py's registry comment on current main, which independently states 'EmailThread aiScore (gmail_service) 723. Next genuinely-free id: 724' — the offers branch's chosen id (724) is exactly the id main's own registry calls free, and a repo-wide grep for 7420240724 across all branches finds it used only on fix/mv-e-offer. No collision. The DDL in 0025_offers.sql is byte-for-byte identical (column-for-column) to the live runtime DDL in services/offers.py::_ensure_offers_table(), so the documentation mirror is accurate (this repo has no migration runner — ADR-TR-1 — lazy idempotent DDL is authoritative, consistent with BackgroundJob/Billing/AdminAuditLog precedent).
coach_honest: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE: services/offers.py compute_suggested_counter() returns None when no offer has a positive base (never $0); _negotiation() returns an honest 'Add an offer with a base salary...' insight and suggestedCounter: null in that case. NegotiationCoach.tsx renders an 'add an offer with a base salary' message and testid=suggested-counter text (never containing '$0') when counter is null, and hides the draft-counter-email button/content entirely in that state. Ran negotiation-coach.test.tsx fresh (vitest): both the null-state (no $0, no draft button/panel) and the computed-state (exact $204,000/$231,000 math for single/multi-offer cases, no fabricated recruiter name/city/role — 'Emma'/'Sydney'/'Senior TPM' replaced with generic '[Hiring Manager]'/'this role' placeholders) pass. Backend tests independently assert the exact expected counter values (204000 for base 185000, 231000 for top base 210000 among two offers), so the math is verified, not just presence-checked.
modal_full_viewport: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE: AddOfferModal.tsx now renders via createPortal(modal, document.body) instead of being a child of the offers page's `space-y-6` container. Root-caused in the diff's own comment: space-y-6 was injecting margin-top:24px onto the modal's `fixed inset-0` root, leaving a 24px strip of the real Topbar clickable behind the backdrop — this is the exact MV-offer-comparison-003 defect. Fresh vitest run of add-offer-modal.test.tsx confirms the portaled node's parentElement is document.body (escaping the space-y flow) and retains the fixed/inset-0 classes needed for full-viewport coverage.
weights_removed: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE: apps/web/src/components/offers/PriorityWeights.tsx is deleted (git diff shows `deleted file mode`); grep for 'PriorityWeights' across apps/web/src returns zero remaining references (no dangling import). page.tsx's subtitle changed from 'Weighted decision analysis across your live offers.' to 'Compare your live offers side by side.' and EmptyState's copy dropped 'weighted decision analysis'/'unlock side-by-side scoring' language — matches the removed functionality, no decorative-but-claimed-functional UI remains. offers-page.test.tsx fresh-run asserts the old claim string is absent and testid=priority-weights is not rendered.
currency_shown: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE (MV-offer-comparison-006): OfferCard.tsx now renders 'Total comp / yr · {offer.currency}'; backend Offer type carries a real user-selected currency (whitelisted server-side to a fixed ISO set, never a fabricated default badge beyond the explicit 'AUD' fallback when the user doesn't pick one). offer-card.test.tsx fresh-run confirms the currency code text is present.
delete_ownership_checked: VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE (MV-offer-comparison-005, partial/DELETE scope): delete_offer(uid, offer_id) executes `DELETE FROM "Offer" WHERE "id" = %s AND "userId" = %s` in one atomic statement (no separate SELECT-then-DELETE TOCTOU window) and returns rowcount>0; the router raises 404 (not 403) whenever rowcount is 0, whether the id doesn't exist at all or belongs to another user — correct anti-enumeration (a wrong-user guess cannot be distinguished from a nonexistent id). test_delete_other_users_offer_is_404 and test_delete_unknown_offer_is_404 both pass and both assert exactly 404.
backend_tests_result: PASS — 19/19 in test_offers_persist.py + test_workspaces.py (targeted run); 17/17 when filtered `-k offer` across the whole api/tests/ tree (includes pre-existing test_offers.py, confirming no regression to the pre-existing derived-offers behavior). Run fresh this session: `DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_TEST AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=false flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock python3 -m pytest tests/test_offers_persist.py tests/test_workspaces.py -q` -> '19 passed, 37 warnings'. Ran against DATABASE_URL_TEST only, never the production DSN.
vitest_result: PASS — 26/26 across src/__tests__/offers/{add-offer-modal,negotiation-coach,offer-card,offers-page,offers-lib}.test.tsx, run fresh this session via `npx vitest run src/__tests__/offers`. Also ran `npx tsc --noEmit -p .` (clean, zero errors) and `npx next build` (clean production build, /dashboard/offers route compiles and is listed in the route manifest) this session.
reasons: All 6 findings in scope (MV-offer-comparison-001/002/003/004/005-partial/006) are addressed at the root cause, not papered over: persistence is a real table+endpoints (not a client-side array), the coach math is computed and disclosed as a heuristic (not a fabricated number), the modal fix is a structural CSS-containment root cause (portal) rather than a z-index/margin patch, and the decorative weights UI was deleted rather than left present-but-inert.

Diffs are minimal and scoped to the offers surface; no unrelated files touched in the two feature commits (b1d7a7f content diff vs 323464d content diff is a no-op except the migration filename rename, confirming the 'finalize' commit did only what its message claims).

Grep across the full diff for @ts-ignore, eslint-disable, Math.random, `: any`/`as any`, TODO/FIXME, and --no-verify found zero hits (the only 'placeholder' matches are legitimate HTML input placeholder= attributes for form hints, e.g. '0', 'e.g. Sydney · Hybrid').

Tests are behavioral, not tautological: backend suggested-counter tests assert exact computed dollar values (204000/231000) rather than merely 'is not None'; user-scoping and cross-tenant-delete tests actually register and authenticate a second real user via /auth/register + /auth/login and assert on that second user's independent session, rather than mocking ownership.

Migration numbering and advisory-lock id were both cross-checked against the CURRENT state of main (which has moved past this branch's merge-base to include the email-center fix's 0024 migration and lock id 723) and found genuinely free — the fixer's own code comment shows this cross-check was done deliberately, and independent re-verification in this review confirms it holds.
concerns: MEDIUM (non-blocking to the defect fixes, but must be cleaned up before merge): commit b1d7a7f accidentally committed a tracked symlink at apps/web/node_modules -> /home/ubuntu/github_repos/aether-job-career-agent/apps/web/node_modules (git ls-tree shows mode 120000, blob 739c2de), carried through unchanged into the final commit 323464d. Top-level .gitignore already has `node_modules/`, so this could only have been added with a forced `git add -f` (or added before ignore rules applied) — it is out of scope for the offer-comparison fix and appears to be an artifact of this VM's convention of symlinking each agent worktree's node_modules to the main repo's real node_modules to save install time (confirmed: an unrelated worktree, agent-a810ef1c7dc96cebe, has the identical symlink but correctly UNTRACKED — `git ls-files` returns nothing, `git status` shows `??`). Because the committed symlink target is an absolute, machine-specific path, a fresh clone or CI checkout of this branch would materialize a dangling symlink at apps/web/node_modules, which would very likely break `npm install`/`npm ci` on any machine other than this VM. Recommend: before merging to main, `git rm --cached apps/web/node_modules` in a follow-up commit on fix/mv-e-offer (not amending 323464d — create a new commit) so main never inherits it.

MINOR: apps/web/src/components/offers/offers-lib.ts still exports WEIGHT_COLORS, weightColor(), and sumWeights() (lines 22-35), which were PriorityWeights.tsx's only production consumers; that component is now deleted, so these three exports are dead production code (they remain referenced only by the pre-existing, untouched offers-lib.test.ts). Not a prohibited pattern and not reachable by users, but it is incomplete cleanup of MV-offer-comparison-004's removal — worth a follow-up trim so the 'weights' concept doesn't linger as unused surface area.

MINOR: the OffersPayload.weights field and the GET response's `weights` array (services/offers.py _WEIGHTS, lines 91-101) are still computed and returned by the API even though no UI consumes them anymore post-MV-004 (PriorityWeights deleted, page.tsx no longer reads data.weights). Harmless (not user-visible, not a fabricated claim since nothing labels it as functional) but is unused payload weight that could be dropped in a later pass; kept here presumably for API-shape backward compatibility, which is a reasonable call but worth an explicit ADR note if it's meant to stay long-term.

None of the above concerns constitute a fabricated/placeholder-content, suppressed-error, or security defect, and none reduce confidence in the offers_persist_user_scoped / delete_ownership_checked / migration_additive_0025_no_dup findings above, which were independently re-verified with fresh test runs in this review.
